certificate.moduleName: SUSE Linux Enterprise Kernel Crypto API Cryptographic Module
certificate.caveat: Interim validation. When operated in approved mode. When installed, initialized and configured as specified in section 11 of the Security Policy
certificate.description: SUSE Linux Enterprise Kernel Crypto API Module provides cryptographic services to the Linux operating system kernel.

At least 256 bits of entropy in the 384-bit output
The CPU jitter is used as a SP800-90B- compliant entropy source.
KATs for AES in ECB, CBC, CTR, GCM, CCM and XTS modes using 128- 192- and 256-bit key size; encryption and decryption are performed separately.
KATs AES CMAC with 128-, 192- and 256-bits keys, MAC generation.
KATs SHA-1, SHA2-224, SHA2-256, SHA2-384 and SHA2-512.
KATs SHA3-224, SHA3-256, SHA3-384 and SHA3-512.
KATs HMAC-SHA-1, HMAC-SHA2-224, HMAC-SHA2-256, HMAC- SHA2-384, HMAC2-SHA-512. KATs HMAC-SHA3-224, HMAC-SHA3-256, HMAC-SHA3-384, HMAC- SHA3-512.

KAT CTR_DRBG with AES with 128-bit key with DF, with and without PR. KAT CTR_DRBG with AES with 192, 256-bit key with DF, without PR. KAT Hash_DRBG with SHA-256 with and without PR. KAT HMAC_DRBG with SHA-256 with and without PR. KAT HMAC_DRBG with SHA-512 without PR.
KAT EC Diffie-Hellman primitive “Z” computation with P-256 curve.
KAT Diffie-Hellman primitive “Z” computation with MODP-2048 and ffdhe3072.
KAT RSA signature verification with 2048-bit key and SHA-256
DRBG health tests as specified in Section 11.3 of SP 800-90Arev1.
Failure of pre-operational tests or conditional tests. Failure of Entropy source Health Tests Failure of PCT tests.
